title: teucrium canadense var. occidentale
intro: Teucrium canadense var. occidentale, also known as Western Mountain Tea or Western Wood Sage, is a variety of the American Mountain Tea plant, native to North America. This perennial herb has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat infections. The medicinal properties of Western Mountain Tea are attributed to its high concentration of volatile oils and flavonoids, which offer various health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, as well as immune system support. Western Mountain Tea can be prepared as a tea or infused in oil for topical use, and is also used in herbal supplements to promote overall well-being and combat various health conditions.
